steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that TRAPPIST planets are tidally locked, meaning each planet's orbital period equals its rotational period. The correct answer must apply this relationship specifically to TRAPPIST-1e using data from the table.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 6.1 days, and its rotational period is 0.92 days.: Radius is a size measurement, not a time measurement — it cannot equal an orbital period. ✗
  • Choice B — 1.51 days, which is the shortest rotational period of any of the TRAPPIST planets.: The orbital period cited does not correspond to TRAPPIST-1e in the table. ✗
  • Choice C — 6.1 days, and its rotational period is also 6.1 days.: This correctly pulls TRAPPIST-1e's orbital period from the table and applies the passage's tidal-locking relationship. ✓
  • Choice D — 9.21 days, which is the same as the rotational period of TRAPPIST-1f.: The values given belong to another TRAPPIST planet, not TRAPPIST-1e. ✗
That is the correct answer.
